name: National Credit Fixers
description_short: Alabama-based credit repair firm founded in 1996, offering custom dispute letters, FDCPA violation recovery, and a concrete 25%-removal money-back guarantee.
description_long:
National Credit Fixers has been operating as a credit repair company since 1996, making it one of the longer-tenured firms in the industry. Founded and led by Matt Liistro, the company is incorporated as National Credit Fixer, Inc. and maintains dual offices in Mobile, Alabama and Vernon, Connecticut. Their 29 years in business provides a depth of experience navigating credit bureau dispute processes that newer entrants simply cannot match. The company positions itself under the "No Advance Fees" model, meaning clients are billed monthly for ongoing work rather than upfront lump sums — a consumer-friendly structure required by the Credit Repair Organizations Act.

National Credit Fixers employs certified FICO professionals who begin each engagement with a free consultation and full credit report analysis. From there, they craft custom dispute letters directed at all three major credit bureaus, individual creditors, collections agencies, and courthouse records. Their scope covers the full spectrum of negative items: bankruptcies, foreclosures, collections, late payments, and charge-offs. A notable differentiator is their FDCPA violation identification service — clients whose debt collectors have crossed legal lines have reportedly received settlement checks ranging from $500 to over $1,000, turning what is typically a cost into a potential recovery. Legal representation for collector harassment claims is included in their service at no extra charge.

Pricing is structured in two ways. Their per-item monthly model, available for in-person or referred clients, starts at $45 per month for a single item and scales to $199 per month for eight or more items. Program-based pricing, referenced in third-party review aggregators, offers a 12-month program at $149 per month and a 6-month accelerated option at $199 per month, with a $199 setup fee applicable for non-credit-repair items. The money-back guarantee stands out for its specificity: a 5-day cancellation window after signing, plus a full 100% refund if the company cannot remove at least 25% of negative items within six months of the contract date — a measurable commitment that most competitors avoid making.

The company holds an A+ rating from the Better Business Bureau, though it is worth flagging that NCF is not currently BBB-accredited despite their website's claim to the contrary — a transparency concern. Third-party reviews on BestCompany average 4.2 out of 5 stars across 34 reviews, reflecting genuine score gains of 60–110+ points for satisfied clients. However, recurring complaints about poor communication after contract signing and inconsistent service quality depending on assigned staff suggest a variable client experience. National Credit Fixers is best suited for experienced consumers who understand the credit repair process and prioritize longevity and FDCPA expertise over digital convenience.

pricing.guarantee_details: 5-day cancellation window after signing. Full 100% money-back guarantee if National Credit Fixers cannot remove at least 25% of negative items within 6 months of the contract date.

tiers.name: Pay-Per-Item
features: Starts at $45/mo for 1 negative item, scales to $199/mo for 8+ items
Custom dispute letters to all three credit bureaus
Disputes with creditors and collections agencies
FDCPA violation identification
Free initial credit consultation
No setup fee for standard credit repair items

features: Fixed $149/month rate for structured 12-month engagement
Custom dispute letters to bureaus, creditors, collections, and courthouses
Dispute of bankruptcies, foreclosures, late payments, and charge-offs
FDCPA violation identification and recovery assistance
Legal representation for debt collector harassment
Credit education resources
Post-repair credit building guidance

features: Accelerated 6-month dispute cycle at $199/month
Custom dispute letters to bureaus, creditors, collections, and courthouses
Dispute of bankruptcies, foreclosures, late payments, and charge-offs
FDCPA violation identification and recovery assistance
Legal representation for collector harassment claims
$199 setup fee applies for non-credit-repair items
100% money-back guarantee if fewer than 25% of items removed

pros: 29 years in business — among the most experienced credit repair firms available
Concrete money-back guarantee: full refund if fewer than 25% of negative items removed within 6 months
FDCPA violation identification can generate $500–$1,000+ settlement payments for clients
Legal representation for debt collector harassment included at no extra cost
Free initial consultation with certified FICO professionals
Disputes courthouse records in addition to bureau, creditor, and collections disputes
Monthly hardship giveaway provides free service to one qualifying low-income client
cons: Not BBB-accredited despite website's claim of full accreditation — a credibility concern
No credit monitoring, online client portal, or mobile app for tracking dispute progress
Communication quality reported as inconsistent and unresponsive after contract signing
Service quality varies significantly depending on which staff member is assigned
Pricing is high relative to competitors, particularly for the 6-month program with setup fee
best_for: Consumers with documented FDCPA violations who may be owed collector settlement money
People disputing complex items such as bankruptcies, foreclosures, and courthouse records
Clients who want a seasoned firm with a long operational history and experience-based process
Anyone who requires a specific, measurable money-back guarantee rather than vague promises
diagnosis: Best for clients with complex negative histories who prioritize firm experience and FDCPA recovery over digital tools. Main caveat: post-signup communication is a recurring complaint and digital account management is essentially absent.
services: Free initial credit consultation and full credit report analysis
Custom dispute letters to all three major credit bureaus
Dispute letters directed to individual creditors and collections agencies
FDCPA violation identification and settlement recovery assistance
Legal representation for debt collector harassment claims
Dispute of bankruptcies, foreclosures, charge-offs, and late payments
Credit education resources and post-repair guidance
Credit building strategy following dispute resolution
Monthly free credit repair giveaway for financial hardship applicants
typical_results_timeline: Initial dispute responses from bureaus typically arrive within 30–45 days of the first round of letters. The company's own guarantee is structured around a 6-month window, with meaningful removal progress expected by that point. Clients reporting positive outcomes cite score gains of 60–110+ points over 3–6 months.
